questions.scenario: You're at 90 Beast Gauge. Storm's Path would give 10 more.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: At 90 gauge, using Storm's Path would overcap. Spend gauge with Fell Cleave first, then continue combo.

questions.scenario: Surging Tempest has 10 seconds remaining.
questions.question: Should you use Storm's Eye to refresh?
questions.explanation: Surging Tempest can be extended up to 60s. At 10s remaining, you still have time. Wait until 10s or less to refresh.

questions.scenario: Combat starting. Your Beast Gauge is empty.
questions.question: How do you build gauge?
questions.explanation: Beast Gauge builds from combo finishers (Storm's Path gives 10, Storm's Eye gives 10). Auto-attacks don't generate gauge.

questions.scenario: Surging Tempest fell off during downtime.
questions.question: What's the priority when boss returns?
questions.explanation: Surging Tempest is a 10% damage buff. Getting it back up takes priority over burst.

questions.scenario: You have 50 gauge. Inner Release is in 5 seconds.
questions.question: Should you spend gauge now?
questions.explanation: Save gauge for Inner Release. During IR, Fell Cleave is free, but entering with gauge means more damage outside IR.

questions.scenario: Large dungeon pull. 8 enemies hitting you.
questions.question: What makes Bloodwhetting so strong here?
questions.explanation: Bloodwhetting heals you for each enemy hit by your attacks. With 8 enemies, your AoEs heal massively.

questions.scenario: Vengeance is up. Tank autos hurt.
questions.question: What does Vengeance provide besides mitigation?
questions.explanation: Vengeance reflects damage back to attackers when they hit you. Great for trash pulls.

questions.scenario: You used Rampart. Tankbuster incoming.
questions.question: What pairs well with Rampart?
questions.explanation: Bloodwhetting adds more mitigation and heals through the hit. Good pairing without wasting bigger CDs.

questions.scenario: Bloodwhetting isn't available yet. Raw Intuition is.
questions.question: What's the difference between them?
questions.explanation: Raw Intuition is the earlier version with the same mitigation but less healing. Bloodwhetting upgrades it.

questions.scenario: Single target boss. Bloodwhetting available.
questions.question: Is Bloodwhetting less valuable in single target?
questions.explanation: Bloodwhetting still provides mitigation and healing in single target. Less healing than AoE, but still excellent.

questions.scenario: You're at 50% HP. Huge tankbuster coming.
questions.question: What happens when you use Holmgang?
questions.explanation: Holmgang prevents you from dropping below 1 HP. You'll take the damage but survive.

questions.scenario: Holmgang has a 240 second cooldown.
questions.question: How does this compare to other tank invulns?
questions.explanation: Holmgang has the shortest invuln cooldown (4 minutes), meaning you can use it more often in fights.

questions.scenario: Holmgang activated. You're at 1 HP.
questions.question: What's the best recovery option?
questions.explanation: WAR has excellent self-sustain. Bloodwhetting heals through attacks, Equilibrium is a strong self-heal. Use everything.

questions.scenario: Boss has back-to-back tankbusters 3 minutes apart.
questions.question: Can you Holmgang both?
questions.explanation: Holmgang is 240 seconds (4 minutes). For busters 3 minutes apart, you could use it on both with good timing.

questions.scenario: Holmgang binds you to the target.
questions.question: What does this binding do?
questions.explanation: Holmgang's bind keeps you and the target within range of each other. Usually not an issue, but can affect movement.

questions.scenario: Inner Release is ready. You have 30 Beast Gauge.
questions.question: Should you spend gauge before IR?
questions.explanation: During Inner Release, Fell Cleave costs 0 gauge. Save your gauge for use after IR ends.

questions.scenario: Inner Release active. 3 free Fell Cleaves available.
questions.question: How many Fell Cleaves can you fit in the window?
questions.explanation: Inner Release lasts 15 seconds with 3 free stacks, but you can fit 5 Fell Cleaves with proper GCD timing.

questions.scenario: Infuriate is ready. You have 0 gauge. IR is on CD.
questions.question: What does Infuriate provide?
questions.explanation: Infuriate grants 50 Beast Gauge AND the Nascent Chaos buff, which enables Inner Chaos instead of Fell Cleave.

questions.scenario: Inner Release window. Primal Rend available.
questions.question: When should you use Primal Rend?
questions.explanation: Use Fell Cleaves first during IR (they're free), then use Primal Rend before the buff expires.

questions.scenario: Inner Release just ended. You have 80 gauge.
questions.question: What should you do with that gauge?
questions.explanation: If you have Nascent Chaos from Infuriate, use Inner Chaos (stronger than Fell Cleave). Otherwise, spend gauge normally.

questions.scenario: Big damage phase starting. Thrill of Battle ready.
questions.question: What's the best time to use Thrill?
questions.explanation: Thrill of Battle increases max HP by 20%. Using it before damage means you have more HP to absorb the hit.

questions.scenario: You're at 40% HP. Equilibrium available.
questions.question: What does Equilibrium provide?
questions.explanation: Equilibrium provides a strong instant heal plus a heal-over-time effect. Great for recovery.

questions.scenario: Infuriate ready. You want maximum damage.
questions.question: What should you use Nascent Chaos for?
questions.explanation: Nascent Chaos enables Inner Chaos (single target) or Chaotic Cyclone (AoE). Inner Chaos is your strongest single hit.

questions.scenario: Thrill of Battle and Equilibrium both available.
questions.question: Which order maximizes healing?
questions.explanation: Use Thrill first to increase max HP. Equilibrium heals more when your HP pool is larger.

questions.scenario: You're main tanking. HP is stable at 80%.
questions.question: Should you use Equilibrium?
questions.explanation: At 80% with no immediate danger, you can save Equilibrium. But if damage is coming, using it proactively is fine.

questions.scenario: You have Rampart active. Party raidwide coming.
questions.question: What happens if you use Shake It Off now?
questions.explanation: Shake It Off consumes personal buffs (Rampart, Vengeance, etc.) to increase the party shield strength.

questions.scenario: Bloodwhetting active. DPS is taking damage.
questions.question: What does Nascent Flash do?
questions.explanation: Nascent Flash shares your Bloodwhetting healing with a party member. Your attacks heal both of you.

questions.scenario: Shake It Off is ready. No personal buffs active.
questions.question: Is Shake It Off still useful?
questions.explanation: Shake It Off still provides a party shield without buffs. The buff consumption just makes it stronger.

questions.scenario: Big raidwide in 5 seconds. Vengeance has 3 seconds left.
questions.question: Should you Shake It Off now?
questions.explanation: With Vengeance about to expire anyway, use Shake It Off to convert it into party protection for the raidwide.

questions.scenario: Large pull. Healer is struggling. You have Nascent Flash.
questions.question: Who should receive Nascent Flash?
questions.explanation: Nascent Flash on the healer shares your Bloodwhetting healing, keeping them alive while they struggle to catch up.

questions.scenario: Inner Release in 15 seconds. You have 40 gauge.
questions.question: What's the optimal gauge management?
questions.explanation: Pool gauge to 50-80 before IR. This gives you gauge to spend after IR ends, maximizing damage.

questions.scenario: Primal Rend available from Inner Release. Boss jumps away.
questions.question: How long can you hold Primal Rend?
questions.explanation: Primal Rend can be held for 30 seconds. This allows flexibility for mechanics or raid buff alignment.

questions.scenario: Upheaval is off cooldown. You're mid-GCD.
questions.question: When should you use Upheaval?
questions.explanation: Upheaval is an oGCD. Weave it between GCDs. Don't let it sit off cooldown.

questions.scenario: 3 Onslaught charges available. Inner Release active.
questions.question: How should you use Onslaught charges?
questions.explanation: Use some Onslaught charges during IR for damage, but keep at least one for emergency gap closing.

questions.scenario: Tank swap needed. You're off-tank with IR ready.
questions.question: Should you swap then use IR?
questions.explanation: Provoke first to grab the boss, then use Inner Release. You want to be hitting the boss during your burst.
